page: Amazon Web Services h1.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance featuring 4000 GB of local HDD storage. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the Xen hypervisor. The network configuration provides a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is poor, scoring in the bottom 10% on stress-ng and Geekbench tests. However, multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and ray tracing benchmarks fall into the average range. Memory bandwidth for read-only operations is average, though large-block write bandwidth is weak, landing in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the h1.4xlarge offers a high-capacity local HDD storage profile, making it a practical option for workloads requiring sequential access to large datasets without the premium cost of SSD-based storage. However, the reliance on spinning disks and the weaker single-threaded performance represent significant tradeoffs for latency-sensitive applications.

This instance is designed for workloads such as large-scale data processing, MapReduce jobs, legacy database operations, and static web serving where massive local storage capacity is required but extreme random I/O performance is not critical.
description: Amazon Web Services h1.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 4000 GB of local HDD storage. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz on the Xen hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data reveals a significant performance tradeoff: while multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and static web serving achieve average marks, single-core processing and large-block memory write bandwidth are weak. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-effective for sequential, high-capacity storage needs. It is appropriate for workloads such as distributed file systems, MapReduce, and data processing tasks that prioritize storage volume over high-speed random I/O.
og_description: Amazon Web Services h1.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 4000 GB of local HDD storage, offering a cost-effective profile for sequential data processing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services h1.4xlarge features 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 4000 GB local HDD storage for cost-effective, high-capacity sequential workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized instance featuring high-capacity local HDD storage and balanced multi-core processing for sequential data workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory powered by Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4
4000 GB of local HDD storage for high-capacity sequential data needs
Average multi-core CPU, software compilation, and static web serving performance
Weak single-core processing and large-block memory write speeds
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Xen hypervisor
Appropriate for MapReduce, distributed file systems, and large-scale data processing
